title: What is React's tearing problem in concurrent mode?
description: What is tearing in concurrent rendering?
options: When a component reads from an external store during concurrent rendering, different parts of the UI may show different values from the same store. useSyncExternalStore solves this by ensuring consistent reads
correct_answer: When a component reads from an external store during concurrent rendering, different parts of the UI may show different values from the same store. useSyncExternalStore solves this by ensuring consistent reads

title: Implement a useLocalStorage hook with SSR support.
description: Create a hook that syncs state with localStorage safely.

title: What is automatic batching in React 18?
description: How did batching change in React 18?
options: React 18 batches ALL state updates automatically (setTimeout, promises, native events), not just React event handlers. Previously only updates in React events were batched. Reduces unnecessary re-renders
correct_answer: React 18 batches ALL state updates automatically (setTimeout, promises, native events), not just React event handlers. Previously only updates in React events were batched. Reduces unnecessary re-renders

title: Implement a useReducer-based form state manager.
description: Create complex form state management with useReducer.

title: What is the useImperativeHandle hook?
options: Customizes the ref value exposed to parent components when using forwardRef; lets you expose specific methods rather than the entire DOM node. Example: exposing focus() and scrollTo() without giving full DOM access
correct_answer: Customizes the ref value exposed to parent components when using forwardRef; lets you expose specific methods rather than the entire DOM node. Example: exposing focus() and scrollTo() without giving full DOM access

title: Implement a custom useBreakpoint hook.
description: Create a hook that returns the current responsive breakpoint.

title: What is the React key reconciliation algorithm?
description: How does React use keys during reconciliation?
options: React uses keys to identify which list items changed, moved, or were removed. Same key = update existing, different key = destroy and remount. Without keys React diffs by index, causing bugs with stateful components. Keys must be stable and unique among siblings
correct_answer: React uses keys to identify which list items changed, moved, or were removed. Same key = update existing, different key = destroy and remount. Without keys React diffs by index, causing bugs with stateful components. Keys must be stable and unique among siblings

title: Implement a useQueue data structure hook.
description: Create a hook that manages a FIFO queue.

title: What is React's strict mode double-invocation?
description: Why does StrictMode run effects twice?
options: In development, StrictMode intentionally double-invokes render, effects, and setState updaters to expose impure code and side effects. Helps catch bugs before they manifest. Does NOT happen in production builds
correct_answer: In development, StrictMode intentionally double-invokes render, effects, and setState updaters to expose impure code and side effects. Helps catch bugs before they manifest. Does NOT happen in production builds

title: Implement a React context with selector pattern.
description: Create a context that only re-renders on selected changes.
